findings.evidence: PR line 94 has `(A : Matrix n n ℝ) (ξ : n → ℝ)`; Mathlib provides the general source theorem at `Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.lean:1078`, `theorem dotProduct_transpose_mulVec [Fintype m] (A : Matrix m m α) (x y : m → α)` under `[NonUnitalCommSemiring α]`.
findings.fix: State it at the algebraic level, e.g. `lemma dotProduct_transpose_mulVec_self [Fintype n] [NonUnitalCommSemiring R] (A : Matrix n n R) (ξ : n → R) : ξ ⬝ᵥ (Aᵀ *ᵥ ξ) = ξ ⬝ᵥ (A *ᵥ ξ)`, and specialize it where needed.
findings.issue: `dotProduct_transpose_mulVec_self` is stated only for `Matrix n n ℝ`, although it is just the diagonal specialization of Mathlib's commutative-semiring transpose dot-product identity.

summary: One remaining matrix helper is a real-only specialization of an existing general Mathlib identity, so the API is narrower than the natural algebraic level.
